message:
fix(channel): reduce lock scope in partition controller and handle version wraparound in watch channel

Partition controller (partition.rs):
- Narrow mutex lock scope in partition() and heal() so the lock is
  released before updating stats or emitting evidence, reducing
  contention under high-throughput partition fault injection
- Fix evidence action strings from "partition_create"/"partition_heal"
  to "create"/"heal" to prevent double-prefixing when the evidence sink
  prepends the component name, which produced "partition_partition_create"
- Tighten test assertions from .contains() substring matching to exact
  == equality so tests will catch future prefix regressions
- Add assertions that no "partition_partition_" prefixed actions exist

Watch channel (watch.rs):
- Replace version counter increment (guard.1 += 1) with wrapping_add(1)
  to prevent undefined behavior on u64 overflow after 2^64 sends
- Change all version comparison checks from ">" (greater-than) to "!="
  (not-equal) so that receivers correctly detect changes even when the
  version counter wraps around past zero
- Update doc comment for changed() to say "differs from" rather than
  "exceeds" to match the new semantics
- Add version_wraparound_still_detects_changes test that forces the
  version counter to u64::MAX-1 and verifies two subsequent sends
  are both correctly detected through the wraparound boundary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
